section.heading: Fees for use of the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center

content.p: The Secretary, acting through the Under Secretary for Management 11 So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma. is authorized to charge a fee for use of the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center of the Department of State. Amounts collected under this section (including reimbursements and surcharges) shall be deposited as an offsetting collection to any Department of State appropriation to recover the costs of such use and shall remain available for obligation until expended.

section.sourceCredit: (Aug. 1, 1956, ch. 841, title I, § 53, as added Pub. L. 105–277, div. G, subdiv. B, title XXII, § 2205(b), Oct. 21, 1998, 112 Stat. 2681–809; amended Pub. L. 107–132, § 2(a), Jan. 16, 2002, 115 Stat. 2412; Pub. L. 119–60, div. E, title I, § 5138, Dec. 18, 2025, 139 Stat. 1569.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2025—Pub. L. 119–60 substituted “The Secretary, acting through the Under Secretary for Management” for “The Secretary”.
2002—Pub. L. 107–132 inserted “George P. Shultz” before “National Foreign Affairs Training Center” in section catchline and in text.

note.heading: Reporting on Pilot Program
note.p: Pub. L. 105–277, div. G, subdiv. B, title XXII, § 2205(c), Oct. 21, 1998, 112 Stat. 2681–809, required the Secretary of State to submit, two years after Oct. 21, 1998, a report to Congress on the number of persons taking advantage of the pilot program established under section 4021 of this title and this section, their business or government affiliations, the amount of fees collected, and the impact of the program on the primary mission of the National Foreign Affairs Training Center, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 107–228, div. A, title III, § 318(3), Sept. 30, 2002, 116 Stat. 1380.

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: For definition of “Secretary” as used in this section, see section 5002 of Pub. L. 119–60, set out as a note under section 2651 of this title.
